---
title: "Plan media features without running them"
subtitle: "Inspect OCR, face, CLIP, DINO, and video-audio routes first."
sidebar: cookbook
---

## Use when

You want to know what a media feature would do before installing optional dependencies or touching large files.

```{python}
from natural_features.workflows import available_features, plan_features

media = available_features(
    modality=["image", "video"],
    budget="allow_python",
)
[entry.feature_id for entry in media if entry.feature_id in {
    "video.audio.extract",
    "image.ocr",
    "video.ocr",
    "vision.face",
    "vision.clip",
    "vision.dino",
}]
```

## Plan a route

```{python}
plan = plan_features(
    "video",
    features=["video.audio.extract", "vision.face"],
    budget="allow_python",
)
plan.to_recipe()
```

## Boundary it closes

Planning uses modality tokens and extractor specs. It lets you check feature IDs, input routing, and default parameters without decoding a movie or loading a neural model.

## Strict variant

```python
plan_features(
    "image",
    features=["vision.clip"],
    budget="allow_python",
    feature_params={
        "vision.clip": {
            "execution_mode": "strict",
            "strict_dependency": True,
            "local_files_only": True,
        }
    },
)
```
